formalized_rules.original_text:
Contracted research may
entail confidentiality and restriction on publication.

llm_annotation.subject: Contracted research
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses 'may' which grants an obligation to the subject.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Contracted_Research, Confidentiality, and Publication_Restriction are classes
fol_formalization.explanation: Contracted research obliges the agent to maintain confidentiality and restrict publication.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Their educational visas may also be
cancelled.

llm_annotation.subject: students with educational visas
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the deontic marker 'cannot' and specifies a prohibition (their educational visas may be cancelled), making it a rule.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals with educational visas cannot cancel their visas
fol_formalization.explanation: This rule prohibits cancelling a visa for individuals who have an educational visa.

formalized_rules.original_text:
(2) Continuing full-time postgraduate (PG) students (Master, Doctor and
Diploma), i.e., those who completed at least one semester/term, will be
invoiced for the applicable registration, tuition, and other fees each
semester/term from the second semester/term onwards, which must be
paid in advance and/or fully paid before each semester/term starts,
otherwise they will not be allowed to register.

llm_annotation.subject: Continuing full-time postgraduate students
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains the deontic operator 'must' and specifies an obligation (what must be done) for a clear subject.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Student, PG, CompletedAtLeastOneSemester, PayFeesEachTerm
fol_formalization.explanation: Full-time postgraduate students who have completed at least one semester must pay fees each term.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Writing-off of doubtful accounts will be requested by OFIN and have to be
approved by the President, or their VP designate.

llm_annotation.subject: someone who writes off doubtful accounts
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses 'have to' which indicates an obligation and specifies a clear action (writing-off of doubtful accounts) that needs approval from the President or their VP designate.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall x [(writingOffDoubtfulAccounts(x) implies (requestedByOFIN(x) and (approvedByPresident(x) or approvedByVP(x))))]
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals are actions, OFIN, President, VP are agents
fol_formalization.explanation: Employees must write off doubtful accounts only if the request is made by OFIN and approved by either the President or their designated Vice President.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Sponsors must be invoiced for outstanding
dues and requested to send AIT a promissory note detailing when and
how payment will be made.

llm_annotation.subject: Sponsors
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains the deontic operator 'must', specifies an obligation (invoicing and providing a promissory note), and has a clear subject ('Sponsors').

fol_formalization.deontic_formula: O(Sponsors \* invoiced \* for(dues, outstanding) \* send(AIT, promissory_note) \* detailing(when, payment) \* how(payment))
fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall s [ Sponsor(s)  and  s = sponsor ⇒ (Invoiced(s, dues)  and  Outstanding(dues)  and  Send(s, AIT, promissory_note)  and  Detailing(promissory_note, when, payment)  and  How(payment)) ]

formalized_rules.original_text:
(20) Overdue accounts shall be reviewed periodically, and follow-up
collections shall be initiated.

llm_annotation.subject: Overdue accounts
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains a deontic operator 'shall' and specifies an obligation to review overdue accounts periodically and initiate follow-up collections.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall t, (OverdueAccount(t)  implies  (At(t, review_overdue_accounts)  and  Exists(t1, Before(t, t1)  and  At(t1, follow_up_collections))))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: OverdueAccount and review_overdue_accounts, follow_up_collections are classes
fol_formalization.explanation: The obligation states that for all times t, if an account is overdue at time t, then it must be reviewed at that time and a follow-up collection must be initiated before the current time.

formalized_rules.original_text:
(10) Additional payments for audited and repeated courses, as well as beyond
the minimum 3 credits for part-time students, registered for the
semester/term must be fully paid one week before the mid-
semester/term examination period starts.

llm_annotation.subject: students who register for additional payments
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains the deontic operator 'must' and specifies an obligation (payments must be paid) with a clear subject (students).

fol_formalization.deontic_formula: O(Paid(x) β (Audited(x) or Repeated(x) or Credits(x) > 3 and PartTime(x)) Before MidSemesterExam(x))
fol_formalization.fol_expansion:
∀x [Payment(x) ↔ Paid(x)] 
∀x [Audited(x) ↔ AuditCheck(x)] 
∀x [Repeated(x) ↔ Retake(x)] 
∀x [PartTime(x) ↔ PartTimeStatus(x)] 
∀x [CreditCount(x) = Credits(x)] 
O(∃x [Paid(x) ∧ (Audited(x) or Repeated(x) or CreditCount(x) > 3) ∧ PartTime(x)] Before MidSemesterExam(x))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Payment, AuditCheck, Retake, PartTimeStatus, MidSemesterExam are properties of a Student
fol_formalization.explanation: Students must pay for audited or repeated courses, or additional credits beyond the minimum for part-time students before the mid-semester examination period starts.

formalized_rules.original_text:
FB-6-1-1: CREDIT POLICY
notice from the Accommodation Unit, and the tenant is required to
vacate the housing unit within two weeks.

llm_annotation.subject: the tenant
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains the deontic operator 'required' and specifies an obligation for the tenant to vacate the housing unit.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals must vacate their housing unit within two weeks after receiving a notice from the Accommodation Unit.
fol_formalization.explanation: The tenant is obligated to vacate the housing unit within two weeks if they have received a notice from the Accommodation Unit.

formalized_rules.original_text: Students shall not disturb fellow students in the residential areas.

llm_annotation.subject: Students
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses 'shall not', which is a clear prohibition and applies to students.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall s, a (Student(s)  and  InResidentialArea(a)  implies  !((s performs action a)  and  Disturbs(s, fellow_student, a)))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Subject is a student, action takes place in residential area, and disturbs another student
fol_formalization.explanation: This rule prohibits students from disturbing their fellow students within the residential areas.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Exceptions to this may only be
given to those whose exchange program is less than two months.

llm_annotation.subject: those whose exchange program is less than two months
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text specifies a condition for an exception (which can be considered as something that should not normally occur), and it uses the deontic operator 'may' in the context of granting permission, making it an obligation.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals have a program length and can be an exception.
fol_formalization.explanation: One must not grant exceptions to those whose exchange program lasts more than two months.

formalized_rules.original_text:
For subsequent semesters, these
students may opt to reside off-campus.

llm_annotation.subject: these students
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the word 'may' which grants a right or permission.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Students can choose to live off-campus in subsequent semesters.
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule states that students have permission to reside off-campus for subsequent semesters.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Graduating students residing beyond the five day limit (including weekends)
after graduation, without prior approval from the OFAM Director may have
their accommodation unit sealed by the Accommodation Unit.

llm_annotation.subject: Graduating students residing beyond the five day limit
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses 'may have' which indicates a prohibition and specifies an action that graduating students should not take if they exceed the five-day limit.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: ∀s, ∃g (Student(s) ∧ Graduated(g) ∧ s=g ∧ Lives(s, accommodationUnit) ∧ AfterGraduation(g) ∧ DaysSinceGraduation(s, >5) ⇒ SealedAccommodationUnit(accommodationUnit))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Student, Graduated, Accommodated, AfterGraduation, DaysSinceGraduation, SealedAccommodationUnit
fol_formalization.explanation: Students who live beyond five days after graduation have their accommodation unit sealed.

formalized_rules.original_text:
● New students may choose from all the available units when their queuing
number is called.

llm_annotation.subject: New students
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the deontic marker 'may' which grants a right or permission.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: exists student forall unit (queued_number(student, unit)  implies  choose_unit(student, unit))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Student and Unit have queued_number property
fol_formalization.explanation: New students are permitted to choose any available unit when their queuing number is called.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Students may put their names
on the waiting list and live in employee accommodation subject to availability.

llm_annotation.subject: Students
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text grants the right to students to put their names on the waiting list and live in employee accommodation.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Student, WaitingList, and LivingInEmployeeAccommodation are classes with appropriate properties for membership and waiting list status.
fol_formalization.explanation: Students may put their names on the waiting list and live in employee accommodation if a spot is available.

formalized_rules.original_text:
shall be the responsibility of the
guests.

llm_annotation.subject: guests
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains a deontic operator 'shall' which specifies an obligation and has a clear subject 'guests'

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals who are guests must be responsible for the action.
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule states that it is an obligation for guests to be responsible.

formalized_rules.original_text:
A student dormitory may be allocated for a
maximum period of one month, in case of availability to:
a.

llm_annotation.subject: A student dormitory
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text grants the right to allocate a student dormitory for a maximum period of one month.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Student dormitories can be allocated for a maximum of one month if they are available.
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule permits the allocation of a student dormitory for a maximum period of one month, given that the dormitory is available.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Sub-letting rooms may result
in eviction from student accommodation.

llm_annotation.subject: students who sub-let rooms
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses 'may result in' which indicates a prohibition and the action of sub-letting rooms has consequences (eviction).

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: StudentAccommodation and not SublettingRooms
fol_formalization.explanation: In any student accommodation, sub-letting rooms is prohibited.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Students, who will study in
another country as part of the Exchange/Dual Degree program, are required to
vacate their rooms while they study abroad.

llm_annotation.subject: Students who will study in another country as part of the Exchange/Dual Degree program
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses 'required', which is a deontic operator indicating an obligation.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Student, AbroadStudy, and VacatesRoom are classes
fol_formalization.explanation: Every student who studies abroad must vacate their room.

formalized_rules.original_text: Alternative rooms designated for such purpose must be used.

llm_annotation.subject: Alternative rooms designated for such purpose
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains the deontic operator 'must' and specifies an obligation.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Alternative rooms have a specific purpose and entities within those rooms must be in the designated alternative room.
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule states that if an alternative room has a specific purpose, then any entity within that room must be in the designated alternative room.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Students found guilty may face a fine of Baht 2,000, or
the actual costs of the damage caused by smoking or burning.

llm_annotation.subject: Students found guilty
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text specifies a penalty for an action (smoking or burning), indicating a prohibition.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall s, (Student(s)  and  smokes_or_burns(s))  implies  (Fine(s, 2000)  or  Costs(s, actual_damage))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Student, SmokesOrBurns, Fine, Costs
fol_formalization.explanation: Students are prohibited from smoking or burning, resulting in a fine of Baht 2000 or the actual cost of damage caused.

formalized_rules.original_text:
The Grievance Tribunal must seek to ascertain, to the best of its
ability, the facts of the case.

llm_annotation.subject: The Grievance Tribunal
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains a deontic operator 'must' and specifies an obligation for the Grievance Tribunal.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: grievanceTribunal has the obligation to maximize effort in fact ascertainment within a day.
fol_formalization.explanation: The Grievance Tribunal must, at each time, make an effort to ascertain the facts of the case within a day.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Acceptance by an employee of a gift valued at THB 3,000 or more must
be reported by the employee to their Unit Head within 15 days of
acceptance, giving details as to the description of the item, sources and
circumstance leading to the acceptance.

llm_annotation.deontic_marker: Acceptance by an employee of a gift valued at THB 3,000 or more must
llm_annotation.subject: employee
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains the deontic operator 'must' and specifies an obligation for the employee to report the acceptance of certain gifts.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall e, u, d, s, c (acceptanceAmount(e, a) and a >= 3000 and not reported(e, u, d, s, c) implies Within(15, acceptance(e), reportGift(e, u, d, s, c)))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Employee (e), UnitHead (u), description (d), source (s), circumstance (c), acceptanceAmount (a)
fol_formalization.explanation: An employee must report the acceptance of a gift worth THB 3000 or more to their unit head within 15 days of acceptance.

formalized_rules.original_text:
The accomplishments, activities and individual differences of other
people must be respected.

llm_annotation.subject: other people
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains the deontic operator 'must', specifies an obligation (what must be done), and has a clear subject (other people).

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall x, (Person(x)  and  other_person(x)  implies  respects(x))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals are persons and if they are others, they must be respected
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule requires that all individuals who are not the self must be respected

formalized_rules.original_text:
They must
inform the President of all settlements made during the grievance
process.

llm_annotation.subject: They (presumably faculty or staff)
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains a deontic operator 'must', specifies an obligation, and has a clear subject.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals involved in a grievance process must inform the President of any resulting settlements.
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule requires that for all settlements made during the grievance process, it is an obligation to inform the President.

formalized_rules.original_text:
In performing their duties, they
must protect the interests of all members of the AIT community.

llm_annotation.subject: they (performing their duties)
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains a deontic operator 'must' which specifies an obligation and has actionable requirements.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: dutyPerformer is a class of individuals who perform duties within the AIT community.
fol_formalization.explanation: Every individual who performs duties in the AIT community must protect the interests of all members.

formalized_rules.original_text:
The Grievance Committee must be prepared to identify
breaches of the Institute P&Ps, etc.

llm_annotation.subject: The Grievance Committee
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains the deontic operator 'must' and specifies an obligation for the subject 'The Grievance Committee'.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Gc: hasPreparation identifiesBreaches
fol_formalization.explanation: The Grievance Committee must always be in a prepared state to identify breaches of the Institute's P&Ps.

formalized_rules.original_text: Items valued at less than THB 3,000 may be accepted without reporting.

llm_annotation.subject: Items
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the deontic marker 'may' which grants permission and specifies an actionable requirement.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: exists x [Item(x)  and  Value(x, v)  and  v < 3000]  implies  Accept_item(x)
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Item and Value properties are required for accept_item
fol_formalization.explanation: Permission to accept an item if its value is less than THB 3,000

formalized_rules.original_text:
When expressing personal opinions outside the Institute, AIT employees
and students are required to note explicitly that the opinions expressed
are personal and do not necessarily represent the position of the Institute.

llm_annotation.subject: AIT employees and students
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the deontic operator 'required' and specifies an obligation for a clear subject.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: ∀x,y (employee_of(x) ∨ student_of(x) ∧ outside_Institute(y) ⟹ (expresses_opinion(x, y) -> (personal(x, y) ∧ not_Institute_position(x, y))))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals are employees or students. Outside_Institute is a property for events. expresses_opinion, personal, not_Institute_position are properties linking individuals and events.
fol_formalization.explanation: The formalized rule states that all AIT employees and students must note explicitly that their opinions expressed outside the Institute are personal and do not represent the position of the Institute.

formalized_rules.original_text:
39 up to Rangsit and from there
may ride a pick-up, popularly known as "Luth".

llm_annotation.subject: students or individuals
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text grants the right to ride a pick-up, which is indicative of a permission rule.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: ∃l, rides(x, pickup, l) ∧ atLocation(l, Rangsit) ∧ typeOf(l, Luth)
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Person x, Location Rangsit, Vehicle pickup (type Luth), Location l
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy permits a person to ride in a 'Luth' pick-up truck from Rangsit.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Thus, they cannot enroll in any course nor
can they avail of student privileges and benefits during the
suspended period such as use of AIT ID card, access to SIS and
registration record, staying in AIT accommodation, use of Library
and School facilities, discount of children in AITIS, etc.

llm_annotation.subject: students during the suspended period
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text explicitly states that students cannot access various privileges and benefits, which indicates a prohibition.

fol_formalization.deontic_formula: F(enroll_course || use_AIT_ID_card || access_SIS || registration_record_access || stay_in_AIT_accommodation || library_use || school_facilities_use || AITIS_discount || children_AITIS_discount)
fol_formalization.fol_expansion: ∀x, (suspended_period(x) → ¬(enroll_course(x) v use_AIT_ID_card(x) v access_SIS(x) v registration_record_access(x) v stay_in_AIT_accommodation(x) v library_use(x) v school_facilities_use(x) v AITIS_discount(x) v children_AITIS_discount(x)))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals cannot perform these actions during the suspended period
fol_formalization.explanation: During the suspended period, no individual is obligated to enroll in a course nor use any student privileges and benefits

formalized_rules.original_text:
If two or more students compete for a position and they are equally qualified to undertake
the work, preference should be given to the student who is self paying or partially-funded
over one who receives a full scholarship.

llm_annotation.subject: the student who is self paying or partially-funded over one who receives a full scholarship.
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text specifies an obligation (who should receive preference) and has actionable requirements (preference given to certain students).

fol_formalization.deontic_formula: O(If (Compete(s1, s2) AND EquallyQualified(s1, s2)) Then (If (SelfPayingOrPartiallyFunded(s1)) OR PartiallyFunded(s2)) Then Preference(s1) Else If (FullScholarship(s1)) Then Preference(s2))
fol_formalization.fol_expansion: O(∀s1, s2 ((Compete(s1, s2) ∧ EquallyQualified(s1, s2)) → (SelfPayingOrPartiallyFunded(s1) ∨ PartiallyFunded(s2)) → Preference(s1)) ∧ (FullScholarship(s1) → Preference(s2)))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Compete, EquallyQualified, SelfPayingOrPartiallyFunded, PartiallyFunded, FullScholarship, and Preference are classes with appropriate properties.
fol_formalization.explanation: The obligation is to prefer a student who is self-paying or partially funded over one who receives a full scholarship when both students compete for the same position and are equally qualified.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Writing-off of outstanding bills including interest due will be requested by Head-
OFAM and have to be approved by the AIT Management and forwarded to
Finance for action.

llm_annotation.subject: Head-OFAM and AIT Management
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses 'have to', which is a deontic operator indicating an obligation.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall x [(HeadOfAM(x) implies writesOff(x)) & (writesOff(x) implies approvedByAITM(x)) & (approvedByAITM(x) implies forwardsToFinance(x))]
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: HeadOfAM, writesOff, approvedByAITM, forwardsToFinance
fol_formalization.explanation: The Head of AM must write off the outstanding bills, which then needs to be approved by AIT Management and forwarded to Finance.

formalized_rules.original_text:
and for external/sponsored students, authorized documentary proof
of sponsorship must be submitted.

llm_annotation.subject: external/sponsored students
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains the deontic operator 'must' and specifies an obligation for a specific subject.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: External/sponsored students must submit sponsorship proof
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule states that for any student who is both external and sponsored, it is an obligation (must) to submit the documentary proof of sponsorship.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Sports facilities cannot be used for other sporting events aside from the
intended use of the court/field.

llm_annotation.subject: Sports facilities
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the deontic operator 'cannot' to specify a prohibition.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall x [ Facility(x)  implies  not (SportsFacility(x)  and  OtherSportingEvent(y)  and  uses(x, y)) ]
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: A sports facility cannot be used for any other sporting event.
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule states that it is prohibited to use a sports facility for any other sporting event aside from the intended one.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Baht 1,250 is the initial premium for new students of August
Intake, to be renewed in January semester of the following
Medical year; for students studying in January and August semesters,
Insurance the annual premium is Baht 2,500; for students graduating in
May or during the inter-semester, the final premium is Baht
1,250 for standard insurance plan.

llm_annotation.subject: new students of August Intake and other specified students
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text specifies an obligation (what must be done) for certain subjects (students) with a clear actionable requirement (premium payment)

fol_formalization.fol_expansion:
O(InitialPremiumIs1250_AugustIntake) ⟹ ∀s:Student, s.intake = 'August' ⇒ s.premium = 1250 
 O(RenewPremiumInJanuaryFollowing) ⟹ ∃m:MedicalYear, ∃t:Time, t.year = m.year + 1 ∧ Before(t, m.JanuarySemester) ∧ ∀s:Student, s.semester = 'January' ⇒ s.premium = 2500 
 O(AnnualPremiumIs2500_StudyingBothSemesters) ⟹ ∃s:Student, (s.semester = 'August' ∨ s.semester = 'January') ⇒ s.premium = 2500 
 O(NoPremiumForGraduatingMayOrIntersemester) ⟹ ∃s:Student, (s.graduation = 'May' ∨ Within(1, year, s.graduation)) ⇒ ¬s.premium = 2500
fol_formalization.shacl_hint:
Student has properties: intake, semester, graduation, premium
 MedicalYear has property: JanuarySemester
fol_formalization.explanation: This policy rule outlines obligations for student premiums based on their intake and graduation dates.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Finally, they must submit the title
page signed by their advisor and by LC to the AIT Registry.

llm_annotation.subject: Students (implied)
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains the deontic operator 'must', specifies an obligation ('submit'), and has actionable requirements ('the title page signed by their advisor and by LC to the AIT Registry').

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals who are both an Advisor and LC must submit the title page
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule requires individuals who are both advisors and LC to submit the title page signed by them.

formalized_rules.original_text: This linen set and pillow may be kept when leaving AIT.

llm_annotation.subject: This linen set and pillow
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the word 'may' which grants permission.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall x (x is linen_set ⟹ exists y (y is AIT ⟹ exists z (z is leaves ⟹ Not(left(x, y, z)))) ) && forall x (x is pillow ⟹ exists y (y is AIT ⟹ exists z (z is leaves ⟹ Not(left(x, y, z)))) )
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Linen set and pillow are entities that can be kept when leaving AIT.
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule permits taking the linen set and pillow when leaving AIT.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Upon receipt of the retake, the School Dean may decide
to:
● Downgrade by 1 step the grade the student will receive upon repetition
of the exam or resubmission of the assignment/project;
● Give a score of ‘zero’ for the specific exam, project or assignment; or
● Give a failing grade or “F” for the course; or
● Recommend the student for suspension or dismissal.

llm_annotation.subject: School Dean
llm_annotation.reasoning: The School Dean is granted the ability to take certain actions (permission), but these actions can have negative consequences for the student (prohibition)

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: ∀s,r,e,a (SchoolDean(s) ∧ Receipt(s, r) ➔ ¬(downgrade(x, -1)(r, s, e, a) V zero(x)(r, s, e, a) V fail(x)(r, s, e, a)))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: SchoolDean, Receipt, Student, Exam, Project, Assignment, Grade
fol_formalization.explanation: The School Dean is prohibited from downgrading the grade by one step, giving a score of zero, or giving a failing grade upon receipt of a retake.

formalized_rules.original_text:
However, if cheating was proven, the
Department Head and the instructor/invigilator may recommend to the
School Dean to disregard the original examination score and require the
student to submit an equivalent piece of work or retake a different version
of the examination.

llm_annotation.deontic_marker: may recommend to the School Dean to
llm_annotation.subject: Department Head and the instructor/invigilator
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text specifies an action (recommend) that the subject must take if a certain condition (cheating proven) is met.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: ∀s (Student(s) ∧ Cheated(s) → O(Dh(recommend(Dean, disregard(score(s))))) ∧ ∃e (DelegateReevaluation(e) ∧ Invigilator(s) ∧ I(recommend(Dh, e))))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Student, Cheated, Dean, DisregardScore, DelegateReevaluation, Instructor, Recommend, Invigilator
fol_formalization.explanation: The department head and the instructor/invigilator are obliged to recommend to the school dean to disregard the student's original examination score if cheating is proven, and they must also delegate a reevaluation.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Complete the Student Research Expense - Advance (SRA) form which
must be approved by the School and submit to Finance for payment
to student.

llm_annotation.subject: Students
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains the deontic operator 'must' and specifies an obligation for students to complete and submit a form.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall x [StudentResearchExpense(x)  implies  (CompleteSRA(x)  and  exists y [SchoolMember(y)  implies  ApproveBySchool(y, x)]  and  SubmitToFinance(x))]
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: A student must complete the SRA form, get it approved by a school member, and submit it to finance.
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule requires that a student completes the Student Research Expense - Advance (SRA) form, gets it approved by the School, and submits it to Finance for payment.

formalized_rules.original_text:
An Assistant’s contract shall be terminated if, during the period of
appointment, they are (a) not achieving good academic standing after one semester; or (b)
on probationary status.

llm_annotation.subject: An Assistant
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses 'shall' which is a deontic operator indicating an obligation and specifies the action that must be taken (termination of contract).

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Assistant has good academic standing or not on probationary status
fol_formalization.explanation: The assistant must maintain good academic standing or avoid being on probationary status

formalized_rules.original_text:
No member of the AIT community, trustee, faculty, staff, or student, including
outreach program participants, should act deliberately or thoughtlessly to interfere
with the study, work or home environment of other members of the community.

llm_annotation.subject: No member of the AIT community, trustee, faculty, staff, or student, including outreach program participants
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses 'should not' with a clear prohibition intent.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: ∀m, ∃o,w,h (Member(m) ∧ Obligation(o) ∧ DeliberatelyOrThoughtlessly(o) ∧ Affects(o, m, interfere) ∧ Study(o, s) ∨ Work(o, w) ∨ Home(o, h))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Member, DeliberatelyOrThoughtlessly, Affects, Study, Work, Home
fol_formalization.explanation: Prohibits any member from deliberately or thoughtlessly interfering with the study, work, or home environment of other members.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Thai VISA Extension Fees
All Non-Thai students are required to pay Baht 1,900 fee for a VISA extension.

llm_annotation.subject: Non-Thai students
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the deontic operator 'required' to specify an obligation for Non-Thai students.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals who are students, non-Thai and in Thailand must pay a fee of Baht 1900
fol_formalization.explanation: This rule states that all non-Thai students in Thailand have an obligation to pay a fee of Baht 1900 for a VISA extension.

formalized_rules.original_text:
In emergency cases or if any medical emergency
assistance is required, the nurse on duty can be contacted at 02-524-5555.

llm_annotation.subject: nurse on duty
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text grants permission to contact the nurse on duty in emergency cases or if medical assistance is required.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: exists n (Nurse(n)  and  Duty(n)  and  Emergency(s) ⇒ May_Contact(n, 02-524-5555))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Nurse, Duty, Emergency, May_Contact
fol_formalization.explanation: In an emergency, the duty nurse may be contacted at the given number.

formalized_rules.original_text: Proper swim wear is required.

llm_annotation.subject: swimmers
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the deontic operator 'required' to specify an obligation.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals who are swimmers must wear proper swimwear.
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule states that it is an obligation for all individuals identified as swimmers to wear proper swimwear.

formalized_rules.original_text:
All dependents have to complete the health declaration form and get approval
from Generali before the effective date.

llm_annotation.subject: dependents
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains the deontic operator 'have to', specifies an obligation (completing a health declaration form), and has a clear subject (dependents).

fol_formalization.deontic_formula: O(Dependent(x) implies Within(effective_date, Day, CompleteHealthDeclarationForm(x)) && ApprovalBy(Generali, x))
fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall x [ Dependent(x)  implies  (Within(effective_date, Day, CompleteHealthDeclarationForm(x))  and  ApprovalBy(Generali, x)) ]
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Dependent, effective_date, Day, CompleteHealthDeclarationForm, Generali
fol_formalization.explanation: All dependents must complete the health declaration form and get approval from Generali before the effective date.

formalized_rules.original_text:
When getting off at the Don Muang (Bangkok International Airport), you may
take local buses no.

llm_annotation.subject: travelers at Don Muang (Bangkok International Airport)
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text grants the right to take local buses, which is a permission.

formalized_rules.original_text:
OFAM is authorized to undertake random inspections and may lay
sanctions if the unit is found to be dirty and unhygienic.

llm_annotation.subject: the unit
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains a deontic operator 'may' and specifies a prohibition ('lay sanctions') when the condition is met (unit is found to be dirty and unhygienic)

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: OFAM checks if unit is unclean and applies sanctions
fol_formalization.explanation: This policy rule prohibits a unit from being unclean when OFAM is authorized to inspect it.

formalized_rules.original_text:
A special student who is a master or doctoral student studying at another institution and
enrolled at AIT under a Memorandum of Understanding, for a purpose other than obtaining
a regular degree, may be appointed as an assistant subject to the approval of the Dean.

llm_annotation.subject: a special student who is a master or doctoral student studying at another institution and enrolled at AIT under a Memorandum of Understanding
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the word 'may' which grants permission, and it specifies an action (being appointed as an assistant) that can be taken by the subject.

fol_formalization.deontic_formula: P(student(x)  and  special_student(x)  and  studying_at_other_institution(x)  and  enrolled_at_AIT_under_MoU(x)  and  not(regular_degree_seeker(x))  and  master_or_doctoral_student(x)  and  action(y)  and  assistant(y)  and  dean_approval(y, x))
fol_formalization.fol_expansion: exists x[student(x)  and  special_student(x)  and  studying_at_other_institution(x)  and  enrolled_at_AIT_under_MoU(x)  and  not(regular_degree_seeker(x))  and  master_or_doctoral_student(x)]  and  exists y[action(y)  and  assistant(y)  and  dean_approval(y, x)]
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Student, Special Student, Master or Doctoral Student, Enrollment under MoU, Assistant, Dean Approval
fol_formalization.explanation: A special student who is a master or doctoral student studying at another institution and enrolled at AIT under a Memorandum of Understanding may be appointed as an assistant, subject to the approval of the dean.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Furniture and appliances should not be removed from the accommodation
unit.

llm_annotation.subject: students or residents of the accommodation unit
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses 'should not', which is a clear prohibition intent.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals of types furniture or appliances should not be removed from an individual of type accommodation_unit.
fol_formalization.explanation: This rule prohibits the removal of furniture and appliances from any accommodation unit.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Before Graduation: All students must have their advisor approve and sign the
title page of their research report, thesis or dissertation, and then submit the
signed title page to the LC for title-checking.

llm_annotation.subject: All students
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the deontic operator 'must' to specify an obligation for students.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion:
∀s, a, t, r, lc (Student(s) ∧ Advisor(a) ∧ Thesis(t) ∧ s = authorOf(t) ∧ a = advisorOf(s) ∧ r = researchReport(t) ∧ lc = LC 
→ (approvesAndSignsTitlePage(a, s, t) ∧ submitsSignedTitlePageToLC(s, t, lc) ))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Student, Advisor, Thesis, ResearchReport, LC are classes. Student has properties authorOf, advisorOf. Thesis has properties researchReport.
fol_formalization.explanation: All students must have their advisors approve and sign the title page of their research report or thesis before submitting it to the Library Catalog (LC) for title-checking.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Formal procedures
3.1 A complainant may, with or without previous recourse to informal channels,
decide to seek redress through formal grievance procedures.

llm_annotation.subject: complainant
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the deontic marker 'may' which grants permission and specifies an actionable requirement for the complainant.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: ComplainantRole: a type of agent capable of making decisions about formal grievances.
fol_formalization.explanation: The complainant has permission to decide to seek redress through formal grievance procedures, regardless of whether they have previously used informal channels or not.

formalized_rules.original_text:
At the beginning of the course, the instructor should make known
the criteria that will be considered in determining the grade such as class
attendance, class participation, papers, examinations, projects and
presentations.

llm_annotation.subject: instructor
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text specifies an obligation for the instructor to perform a specific action (making known the criteria for determining grades).

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall x, y, z [Instructor(x) \&& CourseStart(y) implies MakesKnown(x, GradeCriteria(z))]

formalized_rules.original_text: No credit shall be granted.

llm_annotation.subject: No specific subject mentioned in the text
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains a clear prohibition ('shall not') and specifies an action that cannot be done ('credit shall not be granted').

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals cannot grant credit
fol_formalization.explanation: This policy rule prohibits any individual from granting credit.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Responsibility centers are required to produce internal systems and
documentation for implementing this policy locally.

llm_annotation.subject: Responsibility centers
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains the deontic operator 'required', specifies an obligation for Responsibility centers, and has actionable requirements.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: A ResponsibilityCenter must produce internal systems and documentation.
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule states that all responsibility centers are obligated to produce internal systems and documentation for implementing the policy locally.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Lessees are required to pay three-month advance deposit to take on the
lease of premises.

llm_annotation.subject: Lessees
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the deontic operator 'required' to specify an obligation for Lessees.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Lessee class has obligation to pay advance deposit
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule states that all lessees are obligated to pay an advance deposit. In FOL, this is represented as a universal statement where for every lessee, if they are a lessee, then they have the obligation to pay an advance deposit.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Student Assistants shall be students in good standing on the effective dates of their
appointment.

llm_annotation.subject: Student Assistants
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses 'shall', which is a deontic operator indicating an obligation.

formalized_rules.original_text: Expulsion shall be imposed for drug abuse.

llm_annotation.subject: individuals who abuse drugs
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains a clear prohibition ('shall be imposed') and specifies an action (expulsion) for a specific subject (individuals who abuse drugs)

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals cannot engage in drug abuse
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy states that it is prohibited for all individuals to abuse drugs.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Students are required to vacate their rooms within
five days (including weekends) after their graduation.

llm_annotation.subject: Students
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the deontic operator 'required' and specifies an obligation for students to vacate their rooms.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Student has graduated if they are no longer enrolled
fol_formalization.explanation: Obligation for every student to vacate their room five days after graduation

formalized_rules.original_text:
In discharging his/her responsibility to implement this Policy, the President may
be assisted by the Campus Environment and Development Committee.

llm_annotation.subject: the President
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the word 'may' which grants permission to the President.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: President, CEDC, Assists
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule states that the President is permitted to be assisted by the Campus Environment and Development Committee.

formalized_rules.original_text:
The appeal should be
addressed to the Vice President for Academic Affairs.

llm_annotation.subject: The person making an appeal
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses 'should', which is advisory but in this context implies an obligation to address the appeal to a specific individual.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals who make an appeal must address it to the Vice President for Academic Affairs.
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule states that every appeal must be addressed to the Vice President for Academic Affairs.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Students disturbing the peace of their surroundings shall receive a formal
reprimand as a first warning.

llm_annotation.subject: Students disturbing the peace of their surroundings
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains a deontic operator 'shall' and specifies a prohibition.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall s, (Student(s)  and  disturbing_peace(s))  implies  cannot(s, disturb_peace)
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Subject is a student who is disturbing the peace
fol_formalization.explanation: This rule states that it is prohibited for students to disturb the peace.

formalized_rules.original_text: Proper sports attire is required for each sports facility.

llm_annotation.subject: each sports facility user
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the deontic operator 'required' to specify an obligation.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals in sports facilities must wear sports attire.
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule requires that everyone who is in a sports facility must wear sports attire.

formalized_rules.original_text:
(vii) Overdue accounts shall be reviewed periodically and follow-up
on collections shall be initiated.

llm_annotation.subject: Overdue accounts
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains a deontic operator 'shall' and specifies an obligation to review overdue accounts periodically and initiate follow-up on collections.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: For all overdueAccount, if overdueAccount is overdue then there exists a time point when not(overdueAccount.overdue) and followUpOnCollections is initiated.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Students may put their names
in the waiting list and live in staff housing depending on availability.

llm_annotation.subject: Students
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text grants the right to students to put their names in the waiting list and live in staff housing.

fol_formalization.deontic_formula: P(Student(x) implies (WaitingList(x) and StaffHousing(y) and Available(y) implies LivingIn(x, y)))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Student, WaitingList, StaffHousing, Available, LivingIn
fol_formalization.explanation: Students may put their names in the waiting list and live in staff housing if the staff housing is available.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Students within the normal study period must pay the semester fee of Baht 188,000
for 12 credits Tuition Fee and Registration Fee in advance and/or fully paid up upon
enrolment but not later than the deadline for adding/dropping of courses, otherwise
they will not be allowed to register.

llm_annotation.subject: Students within the normal study period
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains a deontic operator 'must' and specifies an obligation for students to pay semester fees by a certain deadline.

fol_formalization.deontic_formula: O(Student(x) Within(study_period, yr, true) implies PayFee(x, 188000, '12', 'Tuition Fee and Registration Fee') Before AddDropCourseDeadline(x))
fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall x [Student(x) ^ Within(study_period, yr, true) implies (PayFee(x, 188000, '12', 'Tuition Fee and Registration Fee') Before AddDropCourseDeadline(x))]
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Student, PayFee, AddDropCourseDeadline
fol_formalization.explanation: Students within their normal study period must pay the semester fee in advance or fully up to the deadline for adding/dropping courses.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Last players of the evening must turn off the lights and other electrical
equipment of the court.

llm_annotation.subject: Last players of the evening
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains a deontic operator 'must' and specifies an obligation for the subject to turn off the lights and other electrical equipment.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: LastPlayerOfTheEvening is a class, TurnsOffLightsAndEquipment is a property
fol_formalization.explanation: Every last player of the evening must turn off the lights and other electrical equipment of the court.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Vehicle may be locked or fine may also be
imposed, if needed.

llm_annotation.subject: Vehicle owner or driver
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text implies a penalty (fine) for an action (locking the vehicle), which indicates a prohibition rule.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall x [Vehicle(x)  implies  (Locked(x)  or  not Locked(x)  implies  Fine(x))]

formalized_rules.original_text:
Exceptions may only be given
to those whose exchange program is less than two months.

llm_annotation.subject: those whose exchange program is less than two months
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains a deontic operator 'may' and specifies an obligation (what can be done) with a clear subject

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals with exception status must have a duration of their exchange program less than two months
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule states that exceptions can only be given to those whose exchange program is less than two months. This translates into an obligation for any individual (x) that they are excepted, if and only if, their exchange program duration is less than two months.

formalized_rules.original_text:
For training sessions, prior permission has to be taken from OFAM and
trainer might need to pay for using the facilities.

llm_annotation.subject: trainer
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text specifies an obligation for the trainer to obtain prior permission and may also imply a financial obligation.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Trainers must have OFAM permission and pay for facilities to train.
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule states that for training sessions, it is an obligation (must) for the trainer to obtain prior permission from OFAM and pay for using the facilities.

formalized_rules.original_text:
All
new students are required to attend the orientation seminar.

llm_annotation.subject: new students
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the deontic operator 'required' to specify an obligation for new students.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Subject must be a student and action is attending the orientation seminar
fol_formalization.explanation: All new students are obligated to attend the orientation seminar.

formalized_rules.original_text:
A student found guilty of grave cheating by the School
Dean, the Vice President for Academic Affairs and the Academic Senate may
be dismissed.

llm_annotation.subject: A student found guilty of grave cheating
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses 'may be dismissed', which indicates a prohibition and has clear consequences (dismissal).

formalized_rules.original_text:
Before a student is accepted for enrolment, the Admissions and
Scholarships Unit (ASU) is required to ensure that there is proof of
financial support for the student’s full study program.

llm_annotation.subject: Admissions and Scholarships Unit (ASU)
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains a deontic operator 'required' which specifies an obligation for the ASU.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall ASU, if ASU is AdmissionsAndScholarshipsUnit then (exists student, exists program) (enrolls(ASU, student, program) implies has_proof_of_financial_support(student, program))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: ASU, student, program are classes. enrolls, has_proof_of_financial_support are properties.
fol_formalization.explanation: The obligation rule states that the Admissions and Scholarships Unit must ensure there is proof of financial support for each student's full study program.

formalized_rules.original_text:
accommodation units dormitories not designed for such facilities, will be fined
Baht 2,000 and charged for the services related to the removal of the
appliances.

llm_annotation.subject: students (or occupants) using unsuitable accommodation units
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text specifies a prohibition with clear consequences (fine and charges), making it a rule.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Dormitory has no appliances, or if it does, there is a fine and service charge
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule prohibits accommodation units (dormitories) from having appliances. If they do have appliances, a fine of Baht 2000 and a service charge will be imposed.

formalized_rules.original_text:
In all such
cases, the reservation of the vehicle must be made by a member of the faculty
or a senior administrative staff member in the School or by the Student Union
President to the Motor Pool.

llm_annotation.subject: a member of the faculty or a senior administrative staff member in the School or by the Student Union President
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains the deontic operator 'must' and specifies an obligation (what must be done) for certain individuals.

fol_formalization.deontic_formula: O(reserve(vehicle) if (member_of_faculty(x) or senior_admin_staff(x) in School or studentUnionPresident(x)))
fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall x, (reserve(vehicle)(x) ↔ (member_of_faculty(x)  or  senior_admin_staff(x) in School  or  studentUnionPresident(x)))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals must be either a faculty member, senior administrative staff, or the Student Union President to reserve a vehicle.
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy rule obligates any individual (x) to reserve a vehicle only if they are a member of the faculty, a senior administrative staff member in the School, or the Student Union President.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Student dormitories may be provided for a
maximum period of one month, in case of availability to:
a.

llm_annotation.subject: Student dormitories
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text grants the right to provide student dormitories for a maximum period of one month.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Dormitory has a duration property and is provided to students
fol_formalization.explanation: Permission for providing student dormitories for up to one month if they are available

formalized_rules.original_text: Invoices are required to be settled by the indicated due date.

llm_annotation.subject: Invoices
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses the deontic operator 'required' to specify an obligation.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: forall x (Invoice(x)  implies  (DueDate(x)  implies  Settled(x, DueDate(x))))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Invoice has a property 'due_date' and must have a property 'settled' with the same value as 'due_date'
fol_formalization.explanation: Every invoice has an obligation to be settled by its due date.

formalized_rules.original_text:
In case of over staying guests, special prior permission must be obtained from
the Director, Office of Facilities and Asset Management (OFAM) and a daily rent
will be charged for the room.

llm_annotation.subject: guests who overstay
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains the deontic operator 'must' and specifies an obligation for guests who overstay to obtain prior permission from OFAM and pay a daily rent.

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Guest has permission from OFAM and pays daily rent if overstaying
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy states that it is an obligation for a guest who overstays to obtain special prior permission from the Director, Office of Facilities and Asset Management (OFAM) and pay a daily rent.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Families of non-resident students may not reside on campus if the non-resident student is
off-campus.

llm_annotation.subject: families of non-resident students
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text uses 'may not', which indicates a prohibition, and specifies an action that families of non-resident students cannot do (reside on campus).

fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Individuals x are students, families and locations. Properties include NonResidentStudent, LivesOnCampus, onCampus, ResidentOf, OffCampus.
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy prohibits families of non-resident students from residing on campus if the non-resident student is off-campus.

formalized_rules.original_text: Students may wait for the vans at the Thammasat Road near AIT Gate No.

llm_annotation.subject: Students
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text grants the right to students to wait for vans at a specific location.

fol_formalization.fol_expansion: exists s [Student(s)  and  At(s, Thammasat_Road)  and  Waiting(s)  and  Near(s, AIT_Gate)]
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Subject is a student who waits near the specified gate and location
fol_formalization.explanation: The policy permits students to wait at the Thammasat Road near AIT Gate No.

formalized_rules.original_text:
Writing Placement Test for Registration/Enrolment
All degree program students (including CAS and Certificate leading to Master) must
take the AIT Writing Test (AIT-WT) upon arrival on campus.

llm_annotation.subject: All degree program students (including CAS and Certificate leading to Master)
llm_annotation.reasoning: The text contains the deontic operator 'must' which specifies an obligation for a clear subject.

fol_formalization.deontic_formula: O(student(x) && degreeProgram(x) && (CAS(x) v CertificateMaster(x)) => onCampus(x) => takesAITWT(x))
fol_formalization.shacl_hint: Subject is a student, has degree program, is either CAS or Certificate leading to Master, is on campus, and must take AIT Writing Test
fol_formalization.explanation: All degree program students (including CAS and Certificate leading to Master) must take the AIT Writing Test upon arrival on campus.
